meta.update_rule: Update both this file and ROLLOUT-PROGRESS.md together when phase state changes. This file is the machine-readable projection of the .md; it does not own truth.
meta.reading_order: cursor → direction (principles + non_goals) → phases → deferred_register → active_bridges. Read roadmap_ref for full narrative, §2.5 for execution protocol, §9 for skill routing.

principles: Surface before schema — Wave 1 ships the label envelope on the existing backend; Wave 2 changes the stores. UI/API contract is stable across the boundary.
Additive only in Wave 1 — no deletions, no migrations, no new tables. Everything is a tightening of output shape.
Parity from day one — even Wave 1 (single store) must already distinguish Class A and Class B in the envelope. Wave 2 wires a second authoring path without reshaping surfaces.
Gates are pass/fail, not 'mostly ready' — each phase has a hard exit condition. No partial advancement.
Freeze discipline holds — if Wave 1 or Wave 2 surfaces a real conflict with the frozen ADR set, write a superseding ADR before changing code. Do not patch frozen docs silently.
direction.execution_protocol: Every phase ≥ 1.1 runs: /prd-writer → /lead-architect (EXEC-SPEC scaffold) → /build-pipeline. Skills in §9 of the roadmap are dispatched *by* /build-pipeline, not invoked directly. Phase 1.0 is the only exception (meta-phase, PRD/EXEC-SPEC exempt). See ROLLOUT-ROADMAP.md §2.5.
direction.phase_transition_rule: Exiting Phase N → Opening Phase N+1 requires two documents: (1) Phase N exit gate met, (2) Phase N+1 PRD drafted and approved. No code written for Phase N+1 before its PRD lands.
non_goals: No outbox, no consumer, no projections refactor in Wave 1
No performance optimization of the envelope itself in Wave 1
No external integration (reconciliation layer) — deferred past Wave 2
No Compliance domain work — parallel, out of pilot scope
No pit_cash_observation authoring — taxonomy only in Wave 1
wave_1_exit_criteria: SRC envelope present on every production financial surface (API + UI)
Lint rule red on violations, active in CI
Truth-telling test suite passes (harness I5 subset)
Attribution Ratio renders correctly, distinct from completeness
Open questions Q1–Q4 resolved or explicitly deferred with documented rationale

phase_1_5_scope.only_infra_fix: Fix Preview env vars (vercel env add for 3 missing keys). All other CI/CD gaps deferred to Wave 2.
procedure: 1. Fix Vercel Preview env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY)
3. Run blocking checks: lint, type-check, build — must be green
4. Run advisory suite manually and record results (npm run test:surface, E2E I5-1/I5-2 local)
5. Operator walkthrough on PR Preview URL — pit boss interpretability sign-off
7. Smoke-check hosted app at pt-2-weld.vercel.app after Vercel auto-deploy
8. Record CI/CD caveats in Wave 1 retrospective as Wave 2 prerequisites
phase_1_5_scope.no_migration_needed: Wave 1 is surface-only. No schema changes in Phases 1.1–1.5.

phases.label: Pre-Wave-2 Surface Debt Closure
phases.purpose: Close 12 bare-number currency fields deferred from Wave 1 across 4 DTOs in 3 bounded contexts. No schema changes — surface-layer wrapping only.

group_a_VisitFinancialSummaryDTO.note: completeness 'unknown': /financial-summary view has no gaming-day lifecycle column (DEC-1). Wave 2 must introduce lifecycle-aware projection to emit 'complete'/'partial'.

group_b_FinancialSectionDTO.note: BFF RPC path wraps DB bare numbers. completeness 'unknown' same DEC-1 rationale as Group A.

group_c_MtlEntryDTO.note: Individual committed MTL row is always complete — no partial state. Badge computed on bare row.amount BEFORE financialValueSchema.parse() (badge-order invariant).

group_d_MtlGamingDaySummaryDTO.note: DEC-1: completeness always 'unknown' — mtl_gaming_day_summary view has no gaming-day lifecycle column. DEC-2: max_single_in/out are FinancialValue|null (null = no transactions for direction, per EXEC-SPEC DoD line 682).

wave_2_completeness_refinement_dependenc.statement: Standalone GET /api/v1/visits/{visitId}/financial-summary emits completeness.status: 'unknown' always. The underlying mtl_gaming_day_summary view and player_financial_transaction aggregates have no gaming-day lifecycle column that would signal when a gaming day is closed. Wave 2 must introduce lifecycle-aware projection infrastructure (e.g., a gaming-day-close event or lifecycle column) before 'complete' or 'partial' can be emitted on these fields.
wave_2_completeness_refinement_dependenc.wave_2_action: Design lifecycle-aware completeness projection as part of Wave 2 dual-layer + outbox work. Until then, all visit-level financial aggregates emit 'unknown'.

Outbox emission: trigger-based, shared RPC, or both?

governance_rules.phase_entry: Every phase ≥ 1.1 requires PRD drafted & approved → EXEC-SPEC scaffolded → /build-pipeline executed to exit criteria. Tracker checkbox completion does NOT authorize starting the next phase.
governance_rules.skill_dispatch: Skills are dispatched by /build-pipeline, not invoked directly. See ROLLOUT-ROADMAP.md §9.
governance_rules.frozen_docs: ADR-052–055 are frozen. Violations require a superseding ADR — not patches to frozen docs. Use supersession process.
governance_rules.child_spec_rule: Child specs (PRD-072, PRD-073) supersede named parent workstream scope. Their obligations propagate into the parent's exit gate.
